assessor_1_raw:
## Independent Assessment 1: Credit Card Delinquency Prediction

This design has a strict temporal split with a 3-month gap to prevent near-term leakage, equal tuning effort for both models, preprocessing fit on training only, and an intermediate neural baseline to isolate temporal structure from model capacity. The 3-month gap between feature and label windows is a notable strength — it prevents post-delinquency corrective signals from leaking into features. No methodological violations identified.

Verdict: defense_wins
assessor_2_raw:
## Independent Assessment 2: Credit Card Delinquency Prediction

Temporal split is correctly designed with non-overlapping feature and label windows and a protective gap. The account cohort restriction (24+ months old) ensures sufficient history. Equal tuning effort. The intermediate baseline (neural network on tabular features) is a good ablation design.

Verdict: defense_wins
assessor_3_raw:
## Independent Assessment 3: Credit Card Delinquency Prediction

Solid design. The 3-month gap between feature and label windows prevents late-payment signals from leaking into features. Both models receive equal tuning. The per-account z-score normalization for the sequence model is appropriate. AUC-ROC with PR-AUC and threshold-stratified metrics as secondary is a complete evaluation plan.

Verdict: defense_wins
adjudication_raw:
## Ensemble Synthesis (Run 1): eval_scenario_319

All three: defense_wins. Unanimous.

Verdict: defense_wins
